transcript.text: Hi, Liam.
Nice to meet you.
Yeah, good seeing you.
How have things been?
Things are busy.
How about you?
Things are busy as well, which is good, but finally broke 60 degrees here after we got five inches a few days ago.
So things are good.
Things are good.
We'll take that on you.
Where's home for you?
Massachusetts, like an hour away from Boston.
Oh, okay.
Yeah, yeah.
Central Mass?
Central Mass.
You got it.
I know you're cold up there, probably where you are, right?
South Dakota?
Yeah, I'm in Sioux Falls, South Dakota.
So like where about Central Mass?
I'm very familiar with Boston.
So I, Worcester actually specifically, like right.
Yes, exactly.
Right next to West Boylston, so sort of on the suburb line.
Are you familiar with the Worcester area?
Slightly.
It's been a number of years since I was there, but I've a bunch of shows and such in Worcester Centrum.
Oh, yeah.
Yeah, Palladium.
Yeah, that's a very interesting place.
It's got unique people that go there, and it's a good spot to have a show.
They had this sort of rave with an EDM, but it was the Palladium, and then there was a parking lot.
It was sort of an interesting setup, but very cool.
Yeah, very cool that you've been to the Palladium.
A lot's changed, They're building up every time I go into there.
They're really trying to build it up.
So, yeah.
So, yeah.
To date myself, the last show I think I saw at the Palladium was Linkin Park.
Oh, wow.
That is cool.
That is cool.
When did you go?
What year?
That was a number of years back.
Probably 20 years ago at this point in time.
That's legit.
That was probably epic to see them there.
I'm surprised.
They must have been smaller at that point if they were going to the Palladium.
No, they did a, like, it was when they dropped their remix album they did with Jay-Z, they did a small show tour.
Wow.
That is insane.
Man, I wish I was around for that.
Awesome.
I know last time we spoke, I think you were waiting on...
like, a few different vendors or something to get back to you with quotes.
Yep.
And sort of just, like, kind of brought...
The Vanta, the team, so I'm wondering if it's changed.
Yeah, so we've made a little bit of progress.
I got a little bit of a redirection on some of the priorities.
And last week, I was actually able to move forward with Kanji and Okta because they were a burning priority.
Right now, I'm still trying to sort out malware pricing and things like that.
Effectively, we actually started dropping vendors off the list just because they were not being as responsive at all.
And so we've done that.
I've engaged a reseller to help with some of our malware stuff because they want to go through a reseller.
And so working on that and then hoping...
Probably not this week, but next week, to pivot to the Vanta-Drada decision.
And hoping I can get that one through fairly easily.
But it's still TPD.
Progress has been made, though, so that's amazing.
And I guess, at this point, what reason, being that you obviously used Vanta-Vendor, what would be the reason why you wouldn't move forward, or you can see if you wouldn't move forward to Vanta?
So, one, so there was actually an interesting thing over at Vendor in the last few weeks.
Y'all randomly double-charged them, or actually, randomly charged them for last year, even though we already paid last year, last year, while we were going through the renewal for this year.
And honestly, that's...
That put a bad taste in my mouth because it's, we're still trying to get that sorted out with your guys' billing.
And so, like.
Interesting.
Okay.
Yeah.
So that, like that, you know, is, you know, I know it's not you.
It's not anything you can really do anything with or control.
But, you know, stuff like that is, you know, doesn't look good for you.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Apologies about that.
mean, that's obviously not the experience.
So I started working with you to find a resolution on that.
So, you know, our account team over on the vendor side is trying to work through that.
But it's with your guys' your AP team and, like, we're seeing.
Like, we're still trying to get it sorted out, but it's been, you know, a couple of weeks now that the finance team is like, what the hell?
Why was the CEO's credit card randomly charged from Vanta for this amount?
And, yeah, a bunch of residual from that.
So, like, that's kind of where we are, to be honest.
This week, I won't have much time to focus on this decision.
Probably won't pick it up until next week, mainly because, like, literally, I just onboarded Kanji and Vanta at the end of last week.
And in terms of, like, getting those set up or getting those, like, created, and now I'm going to go set them up and start building stuff out.
So, that's kind of where we are at this point in time.
Um, and then, you know, back to, back to looking at this, uh, hopefully next week, so.
Okay.
Yeah.
And yeah, again, apologies about that.
I mean, obviously not the experience that, uh, we want you to have, but hopefully the team finds a resolution to that.
So obviously there's that.
Anything else that would cause you not to move forward to Vanta?
Uh, I mean, pricing.
It's still pricing.
Yeah.
Okay.
And remind me, where are they at in terms of the pricing?
Less than you guys.
How's that?
plus we're, we're pretty close to tipping the 50 user scale.
If we're, we're not there already.
Um, like this week, we will be there very soon.
And that like greatly changes the, uh, the cost factor for us.
Mm.
Yeah.
And obviously, yeah.
I mean, obviously they're going to be cheaper.
That's typically how they went.
Right.
There's space.
On the price, but obviously I want to work with, hopefully make it a no-brainer.
What pricing do you have right now from us?
I know Eliza had sent over some things.
I just want to make sure we're aligned on that.
Yeah.
Do you not have that in front of It looks like I have the startup.
She gave you the core package for $20,000 a year.
I'm unsure on what headcount that was at.
If that was at the $20,000 $50,000 mark or the $50,000 to $50,000.
Yeah, give me one second.
I just want to make sure of that.
So she gave us core at $20,000 for startup discount.
So that's the, and if we would have signed that by February, it would have been $15,000.
Okay.
Um, obviously that didn't happen.
Um, and then let's
Let's see, yeah, so right now we're, it's 20k and I'm pretty sure that was at the 50 headcount, so.
Yeah, 21 to 50, I see right here, core plus package, that includes the questionnaire automation for 20, yeah, okay.
Yeah.
Maybe I want to make sure we kind of put our best foot forward in terms of the above, you said you're going to be tipping over the 50 scale here, pretty sure.
When are you, when are you planning to go over that, is that like the next month or two, or?
Let's see, let me actually tell you where we are as of today, and see the projection numbers, like are off, because we just basically can't find people fast enough.
Um, is kind of where that is, but do you mean- That's a good problem at me.
Sometimes.
Yeah.
does this not give me a count of people?
Why is this so hard to just get a count of people?
I have another place to get it.
They run already today to give me the- Counting to people.
That's no.
Thank you.
you.
Thank you.
you.
Thank you.
I'm this the least efficiently possible at the moment.
Of course.
Okay.
I mean, this includes non-active people, but...
We're close to 58 people already, actually.
Okay.
Again, that includes non-active people, but that's pretty close.
Yeah, okay.
And I guess from your end, it sounds like obviously there's some things going around out of control as far as the payments of vendor and everything.
mean, do you have a preference at this point in terms of the vendor cost aside that you would want to go with?
But it's still kind of a coin flip otherwise.
And really, it may just come down to pricing.
So pricing for features.
that's where we're at.
Right.
Again, I guess I'm happy to work with you on the price.
My biggest fear is that we're never going to be the cheapest.
So, mean, if it's just cost, they're always going to beat us, right?
So, I mean, I don't want to waste your time.
But if you want to proceed with Vanta, if you have a problem.
I'm happy to work with you to get the 51 to 100 scale down to a reasonable number.
can throw a number out there.
Obviously, I want to be a very competitive offer.
So I guess happy to work with you, but if it's just going to come down to cost, I guess that's my hesitation.
Yeah, and where the pricing is right now, because I think you guys quoted me over 30k for, and I've got it somewhere in a spreadsheet, for the 50 to 100.
That's really not going to fly.
Yeah.
I mean, sub 20 is what's going to fly for real.
And I know that's challenging for you guys.
Okay.
Yeah, I mean, I guess it would just have to be a case like if I'm getting a, you know, say like, because it would be like over a 50% discount.
I just need to go to my finance team.
And, you know, I think the things that would help me in terms of getting that level is just like, hey, you know, they want to go with us.
There's a defined timeline, right?
Those are the two aspects.
But from what I'm hearing, it's like, even if we went sub 20, you wouldn't necessarily go with Vanta.
So that's, I guess that's my concern.
Like, it is the, like, it's the product I know.
It is the easiest for me from an onboarding perspective and all of that, right?
So, like, that is the clear advantage you guys have, right?
So my own biases of knowing your product are mostly, like, positive, right?
And so that's why we're still having these conversations, right?
But, like, where you guys are on pricing right now and then the other experiences I've had recently with you guys are also negative to those things.
And so that's kind of where we are right now.
Okay.

If I was able to essentially match the price right now for the 2150 headcount, you know, for the 51200, right, get you down to 20, is that something that would be competitive that you present in terms of Vanta?
You're still fairly high above what they're offering, right?
But how's this?
How high off are we talking?
Because I'm just trying to help you.
I can get a number, you know, approved, but like I want to make it competitive.
Give me till the end of next week and I will come back to you with, you know, hey, can you get it here?
Okay.
Number, right?
And that'll be my ask.
And then you can come back with, you know, no, that's not reasonable.
Here's what I can get, or sure, here's where we can get.
That would be super helpful.
Happy to work with you there.
And then is the company open to multi-years?
Because that's the other thing at disposal, like a two or three-year?
Yeah, so as of right now, what I've heard is not really unless it's going to be substantial cost savings as a result of it, or items like that.
They're really not looking for multi-year, and even if they do, they want the terms broken up.
You know, not everything charged up front.
Yeah, we wouldn't do that.
It would be for a year, but okay.
It's good to know otherwise.
Yeah, and just overall, in like most of the stuff I've seen, it's like no, but because the discount percentages have been like minimal.
Yeah.
Yeah.
multi-year, so.
Makes sense.
Yeah, I mean, because we were.
Coming in at 50% overall, you know, guess that is one of my levers, right?
Saying that you guys would commit to multi-source getting that level discount, whatever.
So it sounds like they're open to it if it's significant, right?
It would be significant.
Yeah.
Okay.
So why don't we do this?
And why don't we put, you said at the end of next week, you'll know?
Yes.
Okay.
So I'll put a placeholder for, would the 25th work, 24th?
Uh, let's, those days are going to be, so I then go into a, um, little bit of a tunnel with my other things.
So give me one second.
I'll tell you when, when's the best kind.
Um, because I get into a little bit of a tunnel on vendor stuff.
So the 30th, if we're going to meet again, the 30th is the best time to do so.
Okay.
Anything between the 25th and the 29th, I've got other obligations that are going to be consuming 90% of my time.
Sure.
Yeah, I can do the 30th.
Let's see here.
can do around the same time.
can do earlier.
Yeah, the same time works.
Okay, great.
And then assuming that we'll have the numbers, is this something that, say, if we did get to whatever number makes sense, the team would be able to move forward in April?
the reason I ask is our end of quarter, so we can get more aggressive in terms of the timeline?
I'm curious.
I mean, it really depends on how quickly I can get folks committed to move.
But, you know, in terms of Okta and Kanji and, you know, past performance is not indicative of future results.
Yeah.
We...
Basically able to get those, once we had a general pay here, we get to this price, we were able to get those signed within 48 hours.

So that's kind of where it is, and most of that time it's just the only person with signatory authority at this point in time is the CEO, and so it's literally just trying to get his attention to DocuSign stuff.
So it's not the out-of-the-world possibility, but typically it takes two days in terms of the signature.
I mean, that's, I think Kanji we had signed, you know, within a day, and Okta we had signed within two days.
Okay.
You know, it just all kind of depends.
And then it also depends on like the actual MSA itself, and, you know, if we need to go back and forth on anything in there, and whether, and what in there is.
Okay, so allow us to go back and forth on it.
Okay, thanks for that.
Yeah, and if you do find out earlier, if you could do me a favor and let me know.
Yeah, will shoot you an email if anything changes before then or if I haven't asked ahead.
Cool, sounds good.
We'll appreciate your time and, yeah, look forward to reconnecting then.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Bye.
The recording has stopped.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Reconnect and discuss potential Vanta adoption for growthx.ai's compliance needs.](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=5.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [growthx.ai has made progress, onboarding Kanji and Okta; Vanta decision pending](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0)
  - [Pricing remains a key factor; growthx.ai seeks sub-$20k for 51-100 employees](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)
  - [Recent billing issues with Vanta at Vendor have created some hesitation](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=299.0)
  - [Yousef to provide a target price by next meeting; Liam open to negotiation](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=758.0)

## Topics

### Recent Progress and Vendor Selection

  - [Kanji and Okta onboarded last week as burning priorities](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=192.0)
  - [Some vendors dropped due to unresponsiveness](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=210.0)
  - [Engaged a reseller for malware pricing](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=225.0)
  - [Vanta-Drada decision expected to be addressed next week](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)

### Pricing Concerns and Negotiations

  - [Current Vanta offer: $20k/year for 21-50 employees (core package)](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
  - [growthx.ai approaching 58 employees, pushing into 51-100 tier](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Vanta's initial quote for 51-100 tier was over $30k (not viable)](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [Yousef indicates sub-$20k is necessary to be competitive](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=817.0)
  - [Liam open to negotiating, potentially matching $20k for 51-100 tier](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=905.0)

### Billing Issues at Vendor

  - [Unexpected double-charge for last year's payment](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=282.0)
  - [Ongoing resolution attempts with Vanta's AP team](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
  - [Incident has created hesitation and "bad taste" for Yousef](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)

### Multi-Year Contract Possibilities

  - [growthx.ai generally not interested unless substantial savings offered](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=973.0)
  - [Preference for annual payments if multi-year is considered](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)
  - [Liam sees multi-year as potential lever for significant discounts](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

### Timeline and Decision-Making Process

  - [Yousef to provide target price by next meeting (April 30th)](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=943.0)
  - [If agreeable terms reached, potential for quick decision (48 hours - 2 days)](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1139.0)
  - [CEO is sole signatory, which can impact turnaround time](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1150.0)
  - [MSA review may affect timeline if negotiations needed](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1189.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Yousef to determine and communicate target price to Liam by April 30th](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=943.0)
  - [Reconnect on April 30th at the same time to discuss pricing](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
  - [Liam to prepare competitive offer based on Yousef's target](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=964.0)
  - [Yousef to email Liam if any updates or changes occur before next meeting](https://fathom.video/share/jBicPAcDM9G-jF7R_VqgyU5px42fqZxJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1209.0)

action_items.description: Prepare to discuss competitive pricing for 51-100 headcount based on Yousef's upcoming ask. Be ready with multi-year options.
Determine specific pricing ask for Vanta (51-100 headcount). Email to Liam if ready before 4/30.
